marketFocus: Operation Epic Fury Day 5: US bombed 2,000+ targets in Iran, destroyed 17 Iranian ships — 70% decrease in launch volumes from Iran suggests war may be peaking; KOSPI circuit breaker triggered (-10%), APAC bloodbath extends

Trump Hormuz insurance: US to provide political risk insurance + Navy escorts for tankers through Strait of Hormuz — crude pulled back from highs (WTI $74.56, Brent $81.40); AUD GDP Q4 beat (+0.8% QoQ vs +0.6% exp) ignored amid risk-off
CAD only G10 outperformer vs USD (oil windfall); EUR/USD sub-1.1600 as Trump threatens to cut all trade with Spain; China NBS PMI miss (49.0) but private RatingDog PMI 52.1 (multi-year high)

USD.summary: USD maintains dominant haven premium as Operation Epic Fury enters Day 5 with no ceasefire in sight. US bombed 2,000+ targets in Iran and destroyed 17 Iranian ships — Trump's Hormuz insurance announcement (political risk insurance + Navy escorts) temporarily capped USD gains but the structural bid remains intact. DXY holds at levels not seen since late January 2026. Key catalysts today: ADP Non-Farm (14:15) and ISM Services PMI (16:00) — both could reinforce higher-for-longer Fed narrative.
drivers: Operation Epic Fury Day 5: premier haven + US energy independence advantage
Trump Hormuz insurance: Navy escorts for tankers — reduces tail risk slightly
DXY at late-Jan 2026 highs (98.43-99.38 range); 99.47 Jan 19 high in sight
ADP Non-Farm Employment (14:15) + ISM Services PMI (16:00) today
Rabo: desire for liquidity ensures USD maintains safe haven bid

EUR.summary: EUR/USD slipped below 1.1600 as Trump threatened to cut all trade with Spain and EU energy shock deepens. European bourses had their worst day since Liberation Day (STOXX 600 -3.2%, IBEX 35 -4.8%). EZ Flash CPI came in hotter than expected (Core 2.4% vs 2.2% exp) — stagflationary signal that complicates ECB easing path. EU Commission proposes Industrial Accelerator Act today. Key risk: further escalation in Strait of Hormuz closure could push EUR/USD toward 1.1575-1.1600 (ING target).
drivers: EUR/USD sub-1.1600 — Trump threatens to cut all trade with Spain
EZ Core CPI 2.4% (vs 2.2% exp) — hotter than expected, stagflationary
STOXX 600 -3.2%, IBEX 35 -4.8% — worst day since Liberation Day
Nat Gas Dutch TTF +32% intraday (EUR 65/MWh from EUR 32/MWh Feb 27)
ECB Kazaks: no need to rush rate decision, cautious mood

GBP.summary: GBP remains under pressure despite the UK Spring Statement being a non-event for markets. OBR downgraded 2026 growth to 1.1% (from 1.4%) and inflation view; headroom GBP 23.6bln (exp. 20-25bln). Gilts gapped lower by 54 ticks and fell over a full point further — UK 2yr yield saw its biggest daily rise since May 2025. GBP/USD holds above 1.3300 support but demand is subdued. BoC Macklem speaks today — watch for any spillover commentary on global energy shock.
drivers: UK Spring Statement non-event: OBR cuts 2026 growth to 1.1% (from 1.4%)
Gilts gapped lower 54 ticks — UK 2yr yield biggest daily rise since May 2025
GBP/USD holds 1.3300 support — demand subdued, upside limited
UK RAF base in Cyprus targeted — UK directly involved in Iran conflict
Energy importer: oil/gas shock = inflationary tax on UK consumer

JPY.summary: JPY remains caught between haven demand and USD dominance. USD/JPY price action was choppy around 157.82 — approaching the intervention zone of 158-160. BoJ Ueda spoke (05:00 today) and communicated closely with the government — no new guidance on rate hike path. The energy shock creates a policy dilemma: higher energy inflation could support rate hikes, but the growth shock from Hormuz closure could delay them. 10yr JGB futures declined as inflationary concerns override haven demand.
drivers: USD/JPY 157.82 — approaching intervention zone 158-160
BoJ Ueda (05:00 today): communicates closely with government, no new guidance
Energy inflation dilemma: higher inflation supports hikes, growth shock delays
10yr JGB futures declined — inflationary concerns override haven demand
Japan S&P Global Composite PMI Final (Feb) 53.90 (prev. 53.80) — solid

AUD.summary: AUD GDP Q4 beat strongly (+0.8% QoQ vs +0.6% exp, +2.6% YoY vs +2.2% exp) but was entirely ignored amid the risk-off mood. The data was prior to the RBA's first rate hike in more than two years, limiting its forward-looking relevance. ASX 200 slumped led by commodity-related sectors. China NBS PMI missed (49.0 vs 49.1 exp) — negative for AUD via China trade channel. Private RatingDog PMI 52.1 (multi-year high) provides a silver lining but insufficient to offset risk-off pressure. BoC Macklem speaks today.
drivers: GDP Q4 +0.8% QoQ (vs +0.6% exp) — beat ignored amid risk-off mood
China NBS Manufacturing PMI 49.0 (miss) — negative via China trade channel
China RatingDog PMI 52.1 (multi-year high) — silver lining insufficient
ASX 200 slumped — commodity sectors led lower by Hormuz disruption
RBA rate hike context: GDP data pre-dates first hike in 2+ years

CAD.summary: CAD was the only G10 currency to eke out gains vs USD on Tuesday — the energy windfall from surging crude prices (WTI $74.56, Brent $81.40) provides direct terms-of-trade support. BoC Governor Macklem speaks today (16:00 Frankfurt Time) — key event for CAD direction. Trump's Hormuz insurance announcement partially capped crude gains, but CAD's energy advantage remains intact. Note: Weekly Bias for CAD is Neutral — this Daily Bullish bias does NOT create a High Conviction Setup alignment.
drivers: Only G10 outperformer vs USD — energy windfall from crude surge
WTI $74.56, Brent $81.40 — direct terms-of-trade positive for CAD
BoC Governor Macklem speaks today (16:00 Frankfurt Time) — key catalyst
Trump Hormuz insurance partially caps crude gains — watch for crude direction
US-Canada trade talks 'constructive' — USMCA uncertainty partially resolved

CHF.summary: CHF maintains its role as premier capital preservation destination, outperforming even JPY as the preferred haven. Gold rebounded from $5,000/oz support — haven demand intact. SNB stated it is 'more prepared to intervene in the FX market' but has not deterred safe-haven flows. Morgan Stanley projects EUR/CHF down to 0.87. Switzerland's energy independence and low inflation profile make it the cleanest haven in the G10 during this energy shock. Swiss CPI (Feb) released today — watch for any inflation surprise.
drivers: Premier haven: outperforming JPY as preferred capital preservation destination
Gold rebounded from $5,000/oz support — haven demand intact
SNB: 'more prepared to intervene in FX market' — intervention risk elevated
Morgan Stanley: EUR/CHF target 0.87
Swiss CPI (Feb) today — watch for inflation surprise

NZD.summary: NZD remains the most vulnerable G10 currency in this risk-off environment. NZ Terms of Trade Q4 beat strongly (+3.7% vs -0.7% exp) but was entirely ignored amid the geopolitical shock. NZD declined alongside AUD on risk aversion and high-beta sensitivity, with no domestic catalysts to provide support. RBNZ's dovish pivot creates a domestic rates handicap reinforced by the global risk-off impulse. NZD is entirely at the mercy of global risk sentiment and China-linked headlines.
drivers: Most vulnerable G10 currency in risk-off — first cut in carry unwinds
NZ Terms of Trade Q4 +3.7% (vs -0.7% exp) — beat ignored amid risk-off
RBNZ dovish pivot: OCR to remain at current levels near term
No domestic data today — entirely sentiment-driven
China NBS PMI miss (49.0) weighs via China trade channel

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: T-notes settled flat in two-way trade as eyes remain glued to the Middle East. 10yr yield at 4.056% (+0.4bps). Inflationary angle of the war dominates — energy prices initially surged before Trump's Hormuz insurance capped gains. Fed speakers (Williams, Schmid, Kashkari) all noted policy is well-positioned; too soon to assess Iran conflict impact on inflation.
drivers: T-notes settled flat — inflationary angle of war vs haven demand in balance
US 10yr yield 4.056% (+0.4bps) — bearish flattening continues
Trump Hormuz insurance: Navy escorts for tankers — reduces inflationary tail risk
Fed Kashkari: 1-2 cuts appropriate if inflation cools; war could justify pause
ADP Non-Farm (14:15) + ISM Services (16:00) today — key data catalysts

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bunds fell over a full point to 128.53 low with losses in excess of a full point at most. Dutch TTF Nat Gas +32% intraday. EZ Core CPI 2.4% (vs 2.2% exp) — hotter than expected, complicating ECB easing path. ECB Kazaks: no need to rush rate decision. German Bund supply today.
drivers: Bunds fell to 128.53 low — Nat Gas +32% drives inflationary fears
EZ Core CPI 2.4% (vs 2.2% exp) — stagflationary signal
ECB Kazaks: no need to rush rate decision, cautious mood
German Bund supply today — technical pressure
STOXX 600 -3.2% — worst day since Liberation Day

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures initially gained amid stock slump and briefly returned to 133.00 level, then hit resistance. BoJ Ueda communicated closely with government — no new guidance. Energy inflation dilemma: higher inflation supports hikes, growth shock delays them. Japan Composite PMI 53.90 (solid).
drivers: JGB futures mixed — briefly hit 133.00 resistance before fading
BoJ Ueda (05:00): communicates with government, no new guidance
Energy inflation dilemma: hike path clouded by Hormuz closure
Japan Composite PMI 53.90 (solid) — supports BoJ hike case
USD/JPY 157.82 — approaching intervention zone 158-160

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts gapped lower by 54 ticks and fell over a full point further to 91.14 trough. UK Spring Statement was a non-event — OBR downgraded 2026 growth to 1.1%. UK 2yr yield saw its biggest daily rise since May 2025 (3.611%). DMO gilt issuance GBP 252.1bln for 2026/27 (exp. ~250bln).
drivers: Gilts gapped lower 54 ticks — fell to 91.14 trough (180 ticks down at most)
UK Spring Statement non-event: OBR cuts 2026 growth to 1.1%
UK 2yr yield: biggest daily rise since May 2025 (3.611%)
DMO gilt issuance GBP 252.1bln — technical supply pressure
UK RAF base in Cyprus targeted — direct UK involvement in Iran conflict
